banner: The last 2026–27 spot is decided in the Almería–Málaga promotion play-off final on June 20 — Racing Santander and Deportivo La Coruña are already up.
footnote: The 20 clubs shown are the 2025–26 season's; once the promotion play-off concludes June 20, Racing Santander, Deportivo La Coruña and the play-off winner replace the relegated trio.

teams.name: Deportivo Alavés
teams.firstGameNote: Drew 2–2 at Real Sociedad in their 1930 debut — and came within one golden-goal own goal of the 2001 UEFA Cup, losing 5–4 to Liverpool in one of the great European finals.

lastTitle.clinch: beat Real Sociedad 2–1 at San Mamés on the final day — Liceranzu scoring both — to pip Real Madrid on the head-to-head tiebreaker

teams.name: Atlético Madrid

lastTitle.clinch: came from behind to win 2–1 at Valladolid on the final day, Luis Suárez striking the title-winning goal

teams.lineage: Athletic Aviación (1940 title) → Atlético Aviación (1941) → Atlético de Madrid (1947– )

lastTitle.clinch: beat Real Madrid 2–0 in the Clásico at Camp Nou — Rashford and Ferran Torres — to seal a second straight title with three games to spare

teams.name: Celta Vigo
teams.firstGameNote: Won promotion in April 1936, then waited three and a half years through the Civil War to finally debut — a 3–2 loss at Zaragoza. Three Copa finals since, three defeats.

teams.name: Elche
teams.firstGameNote: Drew 1–1 at Oviedo in their 1959 debut; that first golden era brought a fifth place that still stands as the club record — and an 11–2 evisceration at the Bernabéu.

teams.name: Espanyol
teams.firstGameNote: Beat Real Unión 3–2 at Sarrià on La Liga's very first matchday in 1929 — Pitus Prat scored the league's first-ever goal — and the founding member is still waiting.

teams.name: Getafe
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 3–1 at Zaragoza in their 2004 debut; back-to-back Copa del Rey final defeats in 2007 and 2008 remain the closest the Azulones have come to silverware.

teams.name: Girona
teams.firstGameNote: Led Atlético Madrid 2–0 on debut night at Montilivi in 2017 before settling for 2–2; the 2024 fairy tale peaked at third place and a Champions League debut.

teams.name: Levante
teams.firstGameNote: Drew 4–4 at Espanyol in a 1963 debut played in torrential rain; their only national trophy is the wartime Copa de la España Libre of 1937 — officially recognized 86 years later.

teams.name: RCD Mallorca
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 2–0 at Betis in their 1960 debut; the league table has never read better than third, twice — and 2025–26 ended in final-day relegation.

teams.name: Osasuna
teams.firstGameNote: Walloped 5–1 by reigning champions Betis in their 1935 debut, then avenged it 6–0 in Pamplona the same season — the Civil War, relegations and 90 years of waiting followed.

teams.name: Rayo Vallecano
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 2–1 at Cádiz in 1977 in a match where both clubs were making their top-flight debuts; the pride of Vallecas has never finished above eighth.

lastTitle.clinch: thrashed Racing Santander 5–0 at El Sardinero on the final day, holding off Madrid FC by a single point
beat Cádiz 3–0 at the Bernabéu, then watched title number 36 arrive that evening as Barcelona fell 4–2 at Girona

teams.name: Real Oviedo
teams.firstGameNote: Demolished Barcelona 7–3 at Buenavista in 1933 — still the greatest debut by a promoted club, with a Lángara second-half hat-trick — then returned in 2025 after 24 years away, only to go straight back down.

lastTitle.clinch: beat Athletic 2–1 at Atocha on the final day to retain the title, two points clear of Barcelona
drew 1–1 at Barcelona's Les Corts on the final day — Araujo's seventh-minute header earning the point that won the title by one
won 2–0 at Sevilla — Vicente and Baraja — to clinch with two games to spare

teams.name: Villarreal
teams.firstGameNote: Led the reigning European champions inside three minutes at the Bernabéu in their 1998 debut before losing 4–1; second place in 2008 is as close as the Yellow Submarine has surfaced.
